steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Find the side length of square N
steps.content: The area of square N is 49 square feet. Since area equals side squared, the side of N is $$\sqrt{49} = 7$$ feet.

steps.title: Apply the area scale factor rule
steps.content: When linear dimensions scale by factor $k$, area scales by $k^2$. Since the side of M is 6 times the side of N, the area of M is $6^2 = 36$ times the area of N. Therefore, area of M = $$36 \times 49 = 1,764$$ square feet. That is the correct answer.
